---
title: "Home"
image: images/headshot.jpg

about:
  template: trestles
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 18em
  links:
    - icon: building
      text: University of Gothenburg
      href: https://www.gu.se/en/about/find-staff/laurenyehle
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:lauren.yehle@gu.se
    - icon: orcid
      text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0009-0009-2038-4148
    - icon: mortarboard
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=KpIU8IQAAAAJ&hl=en&oi=ao
---

## About

I am a PhD student in Political Science at the University of Gothenburg. I study the political feasibility of environmental policy. Specifically, I investigate how compensation for industry and inviduals can make ambitious environmental policies more acceptable among both organized interests and the public.

My work combines public opinion research with political economy to understand how governments can design environmentally effective and politically feasible policies. Alongside my dissertation, I work on the ERC-funded project *Fem-LEAD*, studying how natural disasters shape political behavior and institutional and political trust.


## Research interests

- Environmental politics
- Environmental policy acceptance 
- Public opinion
- Climate policy
- Collective action
- Political behavior

## Methods

- Meta-Analysis
- Survey Experiments
- Cross Sectional Survey Analysis
